timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York"), or null if unset.

userEmail.description: Email address of the user the API key belongs to, or null if unavailable.

to.description: Recipient. Either a phone number in international format (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored in international format.

variables.description:
Per-contact substitution values used by campaign templates.
Each key becomes a token usable as `{key}` in a campaign `text`. Lookup is case-insensitive (e.g. `{firstName}` resolves `firstname`).
Built-in tokens always available regardless of variables: `{whatsappname}`, `{displayname}`, `{phone}`, `{mobile}`. Setting a variable with the same name overrides the built-in.
Storage: keys are persisted as text-cell column titles on the audience board, so values flow back via audiences.get under the same names.
Limits: at most 32 keys per contact; values up to 1024 chars; keys must match /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,31}$/.

ack.description: -1=error, 0=pending, 1=server, 2=device, 3=read, 4=played; null when the engine could not determine.

labelIds.description: Full replacement set of label ids for this chat.

error.description: Set when `result === false`. The literal `wa_business_required` signals the connected account is not a WhatsApp Business account; any other value is the underlying engine diagnostic.

result.description: `true` when WAWeb accepted the mutation; `false` when WAWeb refused or the feature is unavailable on this account.

note.description: Free-text note. Empty string clears the note.

to.description: Recipient. Either a phone number in international format (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored in international format.

variables.description:
Per-contact substitution values used by campaign templates.
Each key becomes a token usable as `{key}` in a campaign `text`. Lookup is case-insensitive (e.g. `{firstName}` resolves `firstname`).
Built-in tokens always available regardless of variables: `{whatsappname}`, `{displayname}`, `{phone}`, `{mobile}`. Setting a variable with the same name overrides the built-in.
Storage: keys are persisted as text-cell column titles on the audience board, so values flow back via audiences.get under the same names.
Limits: at most 32 keys per contact; values up to 1024 chars; keys must match /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,31}$/.

to.description: Recipient. Either a phone number in international format (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored in international format.

variables.description:
Per-contact substitution values used by campaign templates.
Each key becomes a token usable as `{key}` in a campaign `text`. Lookup is case-insensitive (e.g. `{firstName}` resolves `firstname`).
Built-in tokens always available regardless of variables: `{whatsappname}`, `{displayname}`, `{phone}`, `{mobile}`. Setting a variable with the same name overrides the built-in.
Storage: keys are persisted as text-cell column titles on the audience board, so values flow back via audiences.get under the same names.
Limits: at most 32 keys per contact; values up to 1024 chars; keys must match /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,31}$/.

audienceId.description: ID of the audience to target. Each audience contact contributes one outgoing message.

from.description: Sender phone in international format (e.g. +14155551234). When omitted, the workspace default sender is used.

mediaCaption.description: Caption for the media attachment. Requires mediaUrl.

mediaUrl.description: Public https URL of an image/video/document to attach.

onMissingVariable.description:
Behavior when a template references a variable that is not present on a contact:
  - `fail` (default): reject the request at create time; no messages are sent. Returns 400 with `data.examples` listing offending contacts.
  - `skip`: accept the campaign; contacts missing variables receive the message with literal `{token}` left in place.

text.description:
Message body. Supports per-contact substitution using single-brace tokens like `{firstName}` or `{product}`.
Resolution rules (case-insensitive, whitespace stripped):
  - Built-ins always available: `{whatsappname}` (WA profile name), `{displayname}` (firstname+lastname fallback), `{phone}` / `{mobile}`.
  - Custom variables: every property attached to a contact via audiences.append_contacts becomes a token of the same name.
  - Unresolved tokens are left as literal `{token}` in the sent message.
Templates use SINGLE braces `{var}` — `{{var}}` is treated as literal text.

data.description: Engines connected to this workspace. Empty when no engine is paired.

connected.description: Whether this engine is currently online and serving requests.

hasSynced.description: Whether the engine has completed its initial history sync.

id.description: Stable id of this engine session (its MQTT presence client id). Same id space as ping's whatsapp_connections[].id.

state.description: Engine-side connection state (e.g. CONNECTED, OPENING, PAIRING). Null when unknown.

stream.description: Underlying WA stream state. Null when unknown.

type.description: Engine kind. "gateway" = a remote, server-side engine (Blueticks 24/7 gateway / baileys pod). "regular" = the user's own WhatsApp Web browser extension.

message.description: Human-readable note (e.g. why the array is empty). Present only when useful.

connected.description: Whether this engine is currently online and serving requests.

hasSynced.description: Whether the engine has completed its initial history sync.

id.description: Stable id of this engine session (its MQTT presence client id). Same id space as ping's whatsapp_connections[].id.

state.description: Engine-side connection state (e.g. CONNECTED, OPENING, PAIRING). Null when unknown.

stream.description: Underlying WA stream state. Null when unknown.

type.description: Engine kind. "gateway" = a remote, server-side engine (Blueticks 24/7 gateway / baileys pod). "regular" = the user's own WhatsApp Web browser extension.

Error.description: Standard error response envelope. Every 4xx/5xx response from the v1 API carries this exact shape.

details.description: Per-field validation issues. Present on 400/422 when the request body failed Zod parsing.

path.description: Dotted path to the offending field within the request body.

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.

requestId.description: Server-assigned request id; pass this when filing support tickets so engineering can trace the failure.

ErrorEnvelope.description: Standard error response envelope. Every 4xx/5xx response from the v1 API carries this exact shape.

details.description: Per-field validation issues. Present on 400/422 when the request body failed Zod parsing.

path.description: Dotted path to the offending field within the request body.

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.

requestId.description: Server-assigned request id; pass this when filing support tickets so engineering can trace the failure.

limit.description: Max participants to return.

searchToken.description: Search token; when set, only participants whose resolved name or phone/JID matches (case-insensitive substring) are returned, and `total` reflects the matched count.

skip.description: Number of participants to skip before the page (default 0).

includeLastMessage.description: Include the last message snippet on each chat row.

includeWithoutName.description: Include chats with no resolved name (default: skipped).

limit.description: Page size (1-200, default 50).

searchToken.description: Search token; when set, only items whose name matches are returned.

skip.description: Number of items to skip before the page (default 0).

limit.description: Page size (1-200, default 50).

searchToken.description: Search token; when set, only items whose name matches are returned.

skip.description: Number of items to skip before the page (default 0).

chatId.description: Filter to messages addressed to this WhatsApp JID. Matches the recipient stored on the queued/scheduled doc — for sends to a phone number, the JID is `<phone>@c.us`.

limit.description: Page size (1-200, default 50).

searchToken.description: Search token; when set, only items whose name matches are returned.

skip.description: Number of items to skip before the page (default 0).

status.description: Filter by lifecycle status: `pending` (accepted, waiting), `confirmed` (WhatsApp accepted — has waMessageKey), `received` (double grey tick), `read` (double blue tick), `played` (voice played), `failed`.

limit.description: Page size (1-200, default 50).

searchToken.description: Search token; when set, only newsletters whose name matches (case-insensitive substring) are returned, and `total` reflects the matched count.

skip.description: Number of items to skip before the page (default 0).

error.description: Human-readable reason older messages could not be loaded from the phone; null when the pull succeeded or the chat is already fully synced.

historyUnavailable.description: True when no older messages could be pulled because the phone reported it has no retrievable history for this chat (or the phone is offline). When true, `added` is 0, `canLoadMore` is false, and `error` carries the reason. This is distinct from a chat that is simply already fully synced (historyUnavailable=false, added=0).

mediaUnavailable.description: Set when the bytes could not be downloaded or never existed. "expired" = WA aged the file out of CDN retention (typically older than ~30 days for documents) and there is nothing to retry. "fetching" = WA is mid-download from its CDN — transient, the server already retried for ~10s; calling again in a few seconds usually resolves to bytes. "awaiting_sender" = WA's CDN does not have the file but has asked the sender's device to reupload it. The reupload only completes when the sender's WhatsApp client is open and online; closed/backgrounded apps block it indefinitely. Tell the user to ask the sender to open WhatsApp; do not poll. "error" = unexpected failure; "no_media" = the message is text-only / revoked / location / vcard, i.e. it never carried media — caller should not have invoked this tool on this messageKey. Absent when bytes were not needed or when the fetch succeeded.

originalQuality.description: Set to false when the bytes returned are NOT the original sender uploaded — i.e. WA released the original from memory and we synthesized from a smaller WA-generated preview (#113). Currently only happens for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a @newsletter chat). When absent or true, the bytes are the genuine original. Consumers can use this to warn users / decide whether to retry from a different source.

url.description: Hosted URL for the media bytes, or null if the engine could not produce one.

chatId.description: Optional WhatsApp JID to scope results to a single chat. Omit to search across all chats.

includeMediaContent.description: Include inline media payload on rows (heavy; default false — use /messages/{key}/media).

limit.description: Max messages to return.

loadFromPhoneIfNeeded.description: When the local store is exhausted, fetch older messages from the phone (chrome only).

messageTypes.description: Filter to specific message kinds. Comma-separated or repeated. Useful for "all PDFs" (`document`) or "images today" (`image`). System events (gp2/revoked/newsletter_notification) are excluded by default unless explicitly listed here.

order.description: Result ordering. asc = oldest-first; desc = newest-first (default).

searchToken.description: Search token; when set, only items whose name matches are returned.

since.description: Lower bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp >= since.

skip.description: Number of messages to skip before the page (default 0).

until.description: Upper bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp <= until.

unmuteAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the chat auto-unmutes. Omit for "forever".

name.description: Display name of the newsletter / WhatsApp channel.

owner.description: Owner JID, if returned by the engine.

PaginatedResponse.description: Offset-paginated response envelope. Every v1 list endpoint returns this shape (inside the `success`/`data` wrapper).

data.description: Page of items; the item shape is endpoint-specific.

limit.description: Page size echoed back from the request.

skip.description: Offset echoed back from the request.

total.description: Total number of items matching the query, across all pages.

variables.description:
Per-contact substitution values used by campaign templates.
Each key becomes a token usable as `{key}` in a campaign `text`. Lookup is case-insensitive (e.g. `{firstName}` resolves `firstname`).
Built-in tokens always available regardless of variables: `{whatsappname}`, `{displayname}`, `{phone}`, `{mobile}`. Setting a variable with the same name overrides the built-in.
Storage: keys are persisted as text-cell column titles on the audience board, so values flow back via audiences.get under the same names.
Limits: at most 32 keys per contact; values up to 1024 chars; keys must match /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,31}$/.

mediaCaption.description: Replace the media's caption. When set alongside `text`, `text` wins.

mediaUrl.description: Replace the attached media URL (https only).

sendAt.description: Reschedule. ISO 8601 datetime (UTC) in the future.

text.description: New text body. Replaces the previous text/caption.

connected.description: Always true — an entry appears only while its presence heartbeat is live.

id.description: Stable id of the live engine session (its MQTT presence client id).

type.description: Engine kind. "gateway" = a remote, server-side engine (Blueticks 24/7 gateway / baileys pod). "regular" = the user's own WhatsApp Web browser extension.

accountId.description: The account (workspace) the API key belongs to.

api.description: Blueticks API server liveness. "ok" means the API server is up and served this request.

message.description: Present only when whatsappConnections is empty; explains the empty state.

whatsappConnections.description: WhatsApp engines currently connected for this account. Empty means no WhatsApp is connected.

connected.description: Always true — an entry appears only while its presence heartbeat is live.

id.description: Stable id of the live engine session (its MQTT presence client id).

type.description: Engine kind. "gateway" = a remote, server-side engine (Blueticks 24/7 gateway / baileys pod). "regular" = the user's own WhatsApp Web browser extension.

includeMediaContent.description: Include inline media payload on rows (heavy; default false — use /messages/{key}/media).

limit.description: Max messages to return.

loadFromPhoneIfNeeded.description: When the local store is exhausted, fetch older messages from the phone (chrome only).

messageTypes.description: Filter to specific message kinds. Comma-separated or repeated. Useful for "all PDFs" (`document`) or "images today" (`image`). System events (gp2/revoked/newsletter_notification) are excluded by default unless explicitly listed here.

order.description: Result ordering. asc = oldest-first; desc = newest-first (default).

searchToken.description: Search token; when set, only items whose name matches are returned.

since.description: Lower bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp >= since.

skip.description: Number of messages to skip before the page (default 0).

until.description: Upper bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp <= until.

mediaBase64.description: Raw media bytes, base64-encoded (or a data: URL). Used when mediaUrl is absent. For a file upload, send multipart/form-data with a `mediaFile` part instead — it is converted to this.

mediaFilename.description: Filename shown on the attachment (type=media). Optional — derived from the URL / uploaded file when omitted.

mediaKind.description: Media kind (image · video · audio · document · sticker · voice · gif). Optional — auto-detected from the URL / content-type when omitted.

mediaUrl.description: Media source URL (https only). One of mediaUrl or mediaBase64 is required for media sends; mediaUrl wins when both are present.

pollAllowMultiple.description: Allow selecting multiple poll options (type=poll). Default false.

pollQuestion.description: Poll question (type=poll). Required for poll sends.

replyTo.description: Wire `key` of a prior message to quote-reply (from MessageResponse.key). Empty string = no reply.

secret.description: Opaque caller id stored on the message `secret` and carried into the WhatsApp messageSecret.

text.description: Text body (required for type=text) or optional media caption (type=media). Up to 4096 chars. Mention a contact inline with `@[Display Name](<jid>)`, e.g. `Hi @[Joni Naor](972544325389@c.us)`.

mediaBase64.description: Raw media bytes, base64-encoded (or a data: URL). Used when mediaUrl is absent. For a file upload, send multipart/form-data with a `mediaFile` part instead — it is converted to this.

mediaFilename.description: Filename shown on the attachment (type=media). Optional — derived from the URL / uploaded file when omitted.

mediaKind.description: Media kind (image · video · audio · document · sticker · voice · gif). Optional — auto-detected from the URL / content-type when omitted.

mediaUrl.description: Media source URL (https only). One of mediaUrl or mediaBase64 is required for media sends; mediaUrl wins when both are present.

pollAllowMultiple.description: Allow selecting multiple poll options (type=poll). Default false.

pollQuestion.description: Poll question (type=poll). Required for poll sends.

replyTo.description: Wire `key` of a prior message to quote-reply (from MessageResponse.key). Empty string = no reply.

secret.description: Opaque caller id stored on the message `secret` and carried into the WhatsApp messageSecret.

text.description: Text body (required for type=text) or optional media caption (type=media). Up to 4096 chars. Mention a contact inline with `@[Display Name](<jid>)`, e.g. `Hi @[Joni Naor](972544325389@c.us)`.

searchToken.description: Search token matched against contact and chat names.

types.description: Restrict results to these kinds. Comma-separated or repeated. Default: all.

path.description: Dotted path to the offending field within the request body.

get.description: List the WhatsApp engines connected to this workspace — one entry per online engine (a workspace with two paired WhatsApp Web tabs returns two entries). Each entry carries its own `id` (the engine session's MQTT presence client id, same id space as ping's `whatsapp_connections[].id`), its `type` (`gateway` or `regular`), and its connectivity status. When no engine is connected, `data` is empty and `message` explains why — this is not an error.

data.description: Engines connected to this workspace. Empty when no engine is paired.

connected.description: Whether this engine is currently online and serving requests.

hasSynced.description: Whether the engine has completed its initial history sync.

id.description: Stable id of this engine session (its MQTT presence client id). Same id space as ping's whatsapp_connections[].id.

state.description: Engine-side connection state (e.g. CONNECTED, OPENING, PAIRING). Null when unknown.

stream.description: Underlying WA stream state. Null when unknown.

type.description: Engine kind. "gateway" = a remote, server-side engine (Blueticks 24/7 gateway / baileys pod). "regular" = the user's own WhatsApp Web browser extension.

message.description: Human-readable note (e.g. why the array is empty). Present only when useful.

historyUnavailable.description: True when no older messages could be pulled because the phone reported it has no retrievable history for this chat (or the phone is offline). When true, `added` is 0, `canLoadMore` is false, and `error` carries the reason. This is distinct from a chat that is simply already fully synced (historyUnavailable=false, added=0).

get.description:
Download the media attached to a WhatsApp message (image, video, document, audio).
Returns either a hosted URL (`url`) or inline `dataBase64`, plus mimetype + filename.

CAVEAT: for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a `@newsletter` chat), the bytes returned are a WA-generated preview JPEG (~7KB) rather than the original you uploaded — WA releases the original blob from memory immediately after the unencrypted upload. The response will include `originalQuality:false` when this fallback is in effect; the caller can warn the user or decide to retry from a different source.

Requires `chats:read`.

parameters.description: The complete WhatsApp message key (`<fromMe>_<chatJid>_<id>[_<participant>]`), e.g. `false_120363426216988013@g.us_3EB0659D13650092D677AD_188450464616609@lid`.
parameters.name: waMessageKey

parameters.description: Optional. Only used to find the message faster — when you pass a bare message id instead of the complete key, `chatId` lets the server rebuild the full key. If you pass the complete `waMessageKey` you can omit it.
parameters.name: chatId

mediaUnavailable.description: Set when the bytes could not be downloaded or never existed. "expired" = WA aged the file out of CDN retention (typically older than ~30 days for documents) and there is nothing to retry. "fetching" = WA is mid-download from its CDN — transient, the server already retried for ~10s; calling again in a few seconds usually resolves to bytes. "awaiting_sender" = WA's CDN does not have the file but has asked the sender's device to reupload it. The reupload only completes when the sender's WhatsApp client is open and online; closed/backgrounded apps block it indefinitely. Tell the user to ask the sender to open WhatsApp; do not poll. "error" = unexpected failure; "no_media" = the message is text-only / revoked / location / vcard, i.e. it never carried media — caller should not have invoked this tool on this messageKey. Absent when bytes were not needed or when the fetch succeeded.

originalQuality.description: Set to false when the bytes returned are NOT the original sender uploaded — i.e. WA released the original from memory and we synthesized from a smaller WA-generated preview (#113). Currently only happens for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a @newsletter chat). When absent or true, the bytes are the genuine original. Consumers can use this to warn users / decide whether to retry from a different source.

post.description:
Send a message immediately to a specific chat. The body is the same FLAT shape as `POST /v1/scheduled-messages` minus `to` (derived from the URL path) and `sendAt` (this endpoint is fire-and-forget). Set `type` to `text`, `media`, or `poll`. The dispatch is direct — no DB row is created; the response carries the WhatsApp wire key under `key`. For scheduled or queue-managed sends use `POST /v1/scheduled-messages` instead. Requires `messages:write`.

### Sending media (`type: "media"`)

Provide the media **one** of these ways:

- **`mediaUrl`** — a public `https://` URL to the file.
- **`mediaBase64`** — the file bytes base64-encoded (or a `data:` URL), inline in the JSON body.
- **Upload a file** — send the request as `multipart/form-data` with the file in a **`mediaFile`** part. See the cURL example below.

If both `mediaUrl` and `mediaBase64` are present, `mediaUrl` wins. Optional: `mediaFilename` (display name) and `mediaKind` (`image` · `video` · `audio` · `document` · `sticker` · `voice` · `gif`; auto-detected otherwise).

> In the Body panel, the **Attach a file** control has a content-type dropdown next to it. Pick `application/json` to inline a small file as base64 in `mediaBase64`, or `multipart/form-data` to upload the raw file (no 10 MB JSON-body limit) — then just press **Send**. The cURL below is the multipart equivalent.

```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.blueticks.co/v1/messages/120363000000000000@g.us" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $BLUETICKS_API_KEY" \
  -F "type=media" \
  -F "text=Here is the file" \
  -F "mediaFile=@/path/to/photo.png"
```

### Mentioning contacts

Mentions are written **inline in `text`** — there is no separate field. Use `@[Display Name](<jid>)`:

```
Hi @[Joni Naor](972544325389@c.us) this is a test
```

mediaBase64.description: Raw media bytes, base64-encoded (or a data: URL). Used when mediaUrl is absent. For a file upload, send multipart/form-data with a `mediaFile` part instead — it is converted to this.

mediaFilename.description: Filename shown on the attachment (type=media). Optional — derived from the URL / uploaded file when omitted.

mediaKind.description: Media kind (image · video · audio · document · sticker · voice · gif). Optional — auto-detected from the URL / content-type when omitted.

mediaUrl.description: Media source URL (https only). One of mediaUrl or mediaBase64 is required for media sends; mediaUrl wins when both are present.

pollAllowMultiple.description: Allow selecting multiple poll options (type=poll). Default false.

pollQuestion.description: Poll question (type=poll). Required for poll sends.

replyTo.description: Wire `key` of a prior message to quote-reply (from MessageResponse.key). Empty string = no reply.

secret.description: Opaque caller id stored on the message `secret` and carried into the WhatsApp messageSecret.

text.description: Text body (required for type=text) or optional media caption (type=media). Up to 4096 chars. Mention a contact inline with `@[Display Name](<jid>)`, e.g. `Hi @[Joni Naor](972544325389@c.us)`.

post.description:
Send a message via WhatsApp. The body is a FLAT object — set the `type` field to one of `text`, `media`, or `poll`; `type` selects which top-level fields are relevant.

**Variants:**

- `type: "text"` — required `text` (1–4096 chars). A rich link-preview card is attached automatically when the text contains a URL. Example body:
  ```json
  {"type":"text","to":"+15551234567","text":"hello"}
  ```
- `type: "media"` — provide the media as `mediaUrl` (HTTPS) **or** `mediaBase64` (base64 / data: URL); `mediaUrl` wins if both are sent. The bytes are uploaded server-side and only a URL is sent on to the engine. Optional `mediaKind` (`image` · `video` · `audio` · `document` · `sticker` · `voice` · `gif`; auto-detected when omitted), `text` (caption), `mediaFilename`. Example:
  ```json
  {"type":"media","to":"+15551234567","mediaUrl":"https://cdn.example.com/x.pdf","mediaKind":"document","mediaFilename":"receipt.pdf"}
  ```
- `type: "poll"` — required `pollQuestion` and `pollOptions` (2–12 items). Optional `pollAllowMultiple` (default false). Example:
  ```json
  {"type":"poll","to":"+15551234567","pollQuestion":"Pizza?","pollOptions":["Yes","No"]}
  ```

All variants accept optional `sendAt` (ISO 8601 ≥10s future, ≤365d) and `replyTo` (wire `key` of a prior message to quote).

See [Sending messages](/docs/messages) for runnable Python / Node / PHP / cURL examples per variant.

parameters.description: Optional key for safe retries. Identical bodies with the same key return the original response; differing bodies return 409.
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

text_with_link.summary: Text with a URL (link preview auto-attached)

tags.description: Account metadata for the authenticated API key.
tags.name: Account

tags.description: Reusable contact lists used as targets for messages and campaigns.
tags.name: Audiences

tags.description: Schedule audiences for paced bulk delivery with pause/resume/cancel controls.

tags.description: Read and manage WhatsApp chats on the connected engine.

tags.description: Read WhatsApp contacts known to the connected engine.

tags.description: Send or schedule WhatsApp messages via the user-messages queue. Each request creates a queued send — set `sendAt` (RFC 3339) to defer delivery, omit it to send immediately. List, retrieve, edit, and cancel queued sends.

tags.description: Register URLs to receive signed HTTPS POSTs on Blueticks events.
